note: These tests should be run in a test branch or test repository to avoid affecting production code

evals.prompt: I've made some changes to the analytics tracking. Can you commit and push them for me?
evals.expected_output: Analyzes the changes, generates a commit message related to analytics (e.g., 'Update analytics tracking'), commits all changes, and pushes to the current branch. Shows confirmation with commit hash and branch name.

assertions.name: commit_created
assertions.description: A git commit should be created

assertions.name: message_relevant
assertions.description: Commit message should reference analytics or tracking

assertions.name: message_concise
assertions.description: Commit message should be under 72 characters

assertions.name: changes_pushed
assertions.description: Changes should be pushed to remote

assertions.name: confirmation_shown
assertions.description: User should see confirmation with branch and commit hash

evals.expected_output: Checks for changes, generates an appropriate short commit message based on what was modified, commits, and pushes. Handles multiple file types appropriately.
evals.test_setup: Make changes to multiple files (e.g., modify a dart file and pubspec.yaml)

assertions.name: all_changes_staged
assertions.description: All modified files should be staged

assertions.name: message_generated
assertions.description: Should auto-generate a message, not ask user

evals.expected_output: If no changes exist, informs user that working directory is clean and doesn't attempt to commit/push. If changes exist, proceeds with commit and push.
evals.test_setup: Test with clean working directory (no changes)

assertions.name: no_empty_commit
assertions.description: Should not create a commit when no changes exist

assertions.name: clear_message
assertions.description: Should inform user that no changes were found
